alerts.title: Ceasefire collapses after US retaliatory strikes on Iranian soil; threat level rises to 9
alerts.summary: US Central Command conducted multiple airstrikes on Iranian missile and drone facilities in Sirik following Iran's attack on a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on June 26. This marks the first direct violation of the April ceasefire MoU on Iranian territory, with six US military aircraft striking four targets including radar and weapons storage sites. Global threat level escalated to 9 (CRITICAL — IMMINENT ESCALATION) per global_metrics; sentiment across US news now 55% negative with "strikes," "ceasefire," and "violence" dominating discourse.

alerts.title: Iran sustains 60% uranium enrichment with 1-2 week nuclear breakout window amid escalation
alerts.summary: Iran continues enriching uranium to 60% U-235 concentration, maintaining a critical 1-2 week estimated breakout capability per global_metrics. IAEA chief announced inspector access to Iranian nuclear sites contingent on final nuclear deal completion; NPR and South Carolina Public Radio confirm deadlock on terms. Escalating military exchanges (cargo vessel attack, US strikes) occur while nuclear diplomacy stalls, creating window for rapid weaponization if ceasefire fully collapses.

alerts.title: Israel escalates strikes on Beirut and southern Lebanon despite US-brokered ceasefire talks
alerts.summary: Israeli warplanes conducted strikes on Beirut and southern Lebanon targets in response to Hezbollah operations, killing three individuals in Lebanon per Al Jazeera and Axios reporting. Escalation occurs amid ongoing Washington negotiations led by Secretary Rubio aimed at restraining Netanyahu from direct Iran confrontation. Incidents cluster at Israel-Lebanon border; tactical-level operations undermine Trump administration's diplomatic framework to prevent regional widening of US-Iran conflict.

alerts.title: US carrier strike groups and amphibious assets positioned across Persian Gulf and Red Sea
alerts.summary: Three US carriers (USS Dwight D. Eisenhower, USS Abraham Lincoln, USS Gerald R. Ford) plus amphibious assault ships (USS Bataan) and three destroyers (USS Carney, USS Mason, USS Laboon) confirmed deployed across critical zones—Red Sea, Arabian Sea, Persian Gulf—per asset tracking. USS Florida submarine in Persian Gulf. Deployment density indicates sustained US readiness posture ahead of potential escalation cycle following ceasefire violation and nuclear program concerns.
alerts.region: Red Sea / Arabian Sea / Persian Gulf / Mediterranean

alerts.title: Telegram OSINT channels flag first ceasefire violation as deliberate Iranian policy shift
alerts.summary: Middle East Spectator Telegram channel (40.1K views) reports CENTCOM announcement characterizing US airstrikes as 'first direct violation of ceasefire on Iranian soil since MoU signed,' signaling deliberate policy reversal by both parties. Rybar and Bellingcat channels amplifying strike confirmation and casualty estimates. High-engagement posts (~40K+ views) across monitored channels confirm widespread awareness; engagement levels and urgent-keyword flags (breaking, airstrike, missile) reflect elevated concern among OSINT community.

analysis_note: Ceasefire framework between US and Iran has collapsed after coordinated violations; nuclear program remains at critical breakout threshold while military escalation accelerates across multiple fronts (Strait of Hormuz, Israel-Lebanon, cyber claims), elevating systemic conflict risk.
